metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `cohort` stamping to the two synthetic DET `_too_big` paths (sizing.py inline dict and pass1.py::_too_big_finding) using the known criterion id set — or explicitly acknowledge them in the plan and state their intended cohort value so the implementer handles them.
findings.finding: Two synthetic DET `_too_big` finding paths bypass the proposed stamping points entirely: `sizing.py::pass1_with_ladder` (lines 253-269) emits findings inline after the size ladder exhausts all models, never calling pass1_chunk; `pass1.py::_too_big_finding` emits container failure findings that never pass through pass1_container. Post-change, these findings will lack a `cohort` key and — under the plan's own missing-cohort-as-unknown rule — will be silently excluded from calibration even though their cohort is deterministically known (a singleton for chunk-ladder findings, the container id set for container failures).
findings.suggested_fix: In sizing.py's `_too_big` dict append `'cohort': [crit['id']]`; in pass1.py::_too_big_finding append `'cohort': sorted(ids)`. Both cohorts are deterministic at the call site.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC4, e.g.: `python scripts/contamination_rate.py tests/fixtures/sample_sidecar.json` produces a numeric rate and the test in `scripts/` or `tests/` asserts it equals an expected value for the fixture.
findings.finding: AC4 ('a small script/query computes a blocking-tier contamination rate') has no proving command — there is no stated concrete check that the script produces correct or even meaningful output.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the contamination rate formula in the plan (e.g. 'contaminated_count / total_count where a finding is contaminated iff its cohort has size > 1 and its criteria ⊊ cohort') co-located with AC4 so the script has an unambiguous spec to implement and test against.
findings.finding: The plan's contamination-rate computation protocol (AC4: 'A small script/query computes a blocking-tier contamination rate from cohort data') is referenced but its definition is never specified: neither what constitutes a 'contaminated' finding (a finding from a chunk with cohort size > 1? a finding whose attributed `criteria` is a strict subset of its cohort?), nor the denominator of the rate (all blocking-tier findings? all findings with non-null cohort?), is defined anywhere in the plan. An executor writing the required script has no formula to implement.
findings.location: AC line 4: 'A small script/query computes a blocking-tier contamination rate from cohort data (committed under scripts/ or a test). Unblocks fort-wisp-wren.'

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan that `cohort` is injected into the raw finding dict POST-parse (inside `out.append(...)` in each finder), NOT added to the `P1Finding` Pydantic schema / `plan_review_findings` contract, so the model is never asked to emit it.
findings.finding: The `plan_review_findings` Pydantic schema (`P1Finding` at passes.py:84-104) does not include a `cohort` field, yet the plan instructs 'stamp each Pass-1 finding with its chunk COHORT' without specifying whether cohort is added to the structured-output model (requiring a schema change) or injected into the raw Python dict post-parse. Since all three finder paths (`pass1_chunk`, `pass1_container`, `pass1_isf`) parse through `output_schema='plan_review_findings'`, an implementer cannot determine from the plan whether the schema itself must grow a `cohort` field or whether a post-parse dict augmentation is correct.
findings.location: What section / passes.py:84-104 (P1Finding schema) / AC line 'cohort (sorted chunk criterion-id set…) stamped on findings'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence under 'What': 'cohort is a CALLER-COMPUTED field injected into the raw dict at `out.append(...)` in each finder — it is NOT added to the `plan_review_findings` Pydantic schema (the model never emits it; the chunk ids are known to the caller before the LLM call).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a 'What' entry describing the contamination-rate script: its inputs (sidecar cohort fields), the computation (e.g. fraction of findings where |cohort| > 1), the definition of 'blocking-tier', and its expected output format, so AC 4 has a traceable specification.
findings.finding: AC item 4 introduces a deliverable — 'a small script/query [that] computes a blocking-tier contamination rate from cohort data (committed under scripts/ or a test)' — that has no corresponding description in the 'What' section body, making it an orphan criterion with no specified design, inputs, or algorithm.
findings.location: AC item 4 vs. 'What' section (all five bullets)
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, add a sixth bullet such as: 'Contamination-rate script (scripts/cohort_contamination.py or equivalent): reads sidecar JSON files, groups findings by cohort, computes fraction of findings where len(cohort) > 1 as the contamination rate, and prints a summary. A rate above [threshold]% is considered blocking-tier for fort-wisp-wren. The script accepts a glob of sidecar paths as argv.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC or test assertion verifying the contamination-rate script skips (treats as 'unknown') findings with a missing `cohort` field, matching the documented fallback stated in the plan body.
findings.finding: AC-4 requires a contamination-rate script but does not require the script to implement (or test) the explicitly-documented missing-cohort fallback (treat missing cohort as 'unknown', skip from rate). The plan body calls this out as critical for correctness, but no acceptance criterion verifies it, leaving a silent data-corruption risk in the very measurement the ticket is meant to enable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC-4; 'What' section — 'Old sidecars / pre-change findings lack cohort' paragraph
findings.suggested_fix: In AC-4, append: 'Script must skip findings where `cohort` is absent and report the excluded-count separately; add a unit assertion (or inline doctest) covering a mixed input with one cohort-bearing and one cohort-missing finding.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen the AC1 jq command to assert non-null: e.g. `jq -e '[.findings[].cohort] | all(. != null)' <sidecar>` (exits non-zero if any cohort is null).
findings.finding: The jq proving command in AC1 (`jq '.findings[].cohort' <sidecar>`) outputs values without asserting they are non-null; if any finding carries a null cohort the command still exits 0 and prints 'null', silently passing the criterion.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the current proving command with: `jq -e '[.findings[].cohort] | all(. != null and (type == "array") and length > 0)' <sidecar>` so the command exits non-zero on any absent or empty cohort.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'blocking-tier' in AC4: state which finding severity label(s) or field value(s) qualify as blocking, the expected output format of the script (e.g. a float 0–1 or a percentage line), and a fixture-based expected value so the outcome is observable.
findings.finding: AC4 uses the undefined term 'blocking-tier contamination rate' without specifying what 'blocking-tier' means (a threshold value, a label on findings, a tier field in the sidecar schema), leaving the executor to choose the design.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the documentation artifact to a concrete path (e.g. `docs/sidecar_schema.md` or a `CLAUDE.md` section) and ensure it covers both the cohort field definition AND the 'missing cohort = unknown' fallback rule for offline analysis.
findings.finding: The plan names a 'sidecar schema note (docs)' but specifies no concrete artifact path, and the behavioral contract 'missing cohort = unknown (skip from contamination rate)' has no designated home that downstream consumers or future maintainers of offline-analysis tooling would find.
findings.location: What section ('Document the cohort field in a sidecar schema note (docs)') and AC item 2 ('offline analysis treats missing cohort as unknown (documented)')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC 2: 'Schema note lives at `docs/sidecar_schema.md#cohort` (or equivalent); the missing-cohort fallback rule is stated there, not only in this story text.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sizing.py` (two `pass1_chunk` call sites at lines ~221 and ~236) to the enumerated callers, and confirm it transparently passes the new `cohort` key through without stripping.
findings.finding: sizing.py contains two call sites for pass1_chunk (lines 221 and 236) that the plan does not enumerate as consumers requiring attention. While both are in the same package directory as passes.py, sizing.py assembles and returns the cohort-stamped findings — if cohort is added to the pass1_chunk return dict, sizing.py transparently passes it through with no changes needed, but the plan's consumer enumeration omits this caller entirely.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Out-of-plan_review callers' bullet, add: 'src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sizing.py (lines ~221, ~236) — also calls pass1_chunk; it extends/returns findings directly so cohort is passed through without modification; no change needed but confirm.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Note in the 'pass1_isf' bullet that its finding dict has a different shape (no `checklist_item`, `suggested_fix`, or `location` keys) — confirm the cohort stamp is added to the ISF-specific output dict and that _slim handles the resulting nulls gracefully.
findings.finding: The pass1_isf output dict (passes.py lines ~451-461) has a materially different shape from pass1_chunk/pass1_container output: it does not include 'checklist_item', 'suggested_fix', or 'location' keys. The plan instructs stamping cohort on ISF findings but does not acknowledge this divergent shape, leaving the implementer to discover the difference at implementation time.
findings.location: What (verified code targets) — 'pass1_isf -- runs as a SINGLE fixed call ... cohort = a singleton ["ISF"]'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the AC1 jq proof command to actually assert non-null, e.g. `jq -e '[.findings[].cohort | select(. == null)] | length == 0' <sidecar>` or `jq -e '.findings[] | .cohort | arrays' <sidecar> | wc -l` compared against total finding count.
findings.finding: The AC1 proof command `jq '.findings[].cohort' <sidecar>` does not verify non-null: jq prints `null` for a missing/null cohort and exits 0, so the command passes even if some findings have no cohort. The proof would silently not catch a regression.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC item 1: 'jq '.findings[].cohort' <sidecar> non-null for all three finder types'
findings.suggested_fix: `jq -e '[.findings[].cohort | select(. == null or length == 0)] | length == 0' <sidecar>` exits non-zero if any finding is missing or has an empty cohort.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for stamping cohort at the finder level: the chunk definition lives at the call site (the LLM literally receives those ids together), the orchestrator only sees the returned findings and cannot reconstruct co-residency after the fact.
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for WHY stamping cohort at the individual finder-function level (vs. at the orchestrator aggregation point in pass1.py::run_pass1 where all chunk findings are collected) is the right boundary. Without this, an implementer cannot reason about whether the aggregation point is a valid alternative and may introduce a second, conflicting stamping location.
findings.location: What section: 'Stamp each Pass-1 finding with its chunk COHORT' — no rationale for finder-level vs. aggregation-level placement
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Cohort is stamped at the finder (not the orchestrator) because cohort = the criterion-ids the model actually saw co-resident; the orchestrator collects results but cannot reconstruct which findings came from the same LLM context window.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
